topic: Diversity of living and non-living things

buckets.description: Questions that require students to use or interpret classification tools like flowcharts, tables, and dichotomous keys to group organisms.
Questions that test knowledge of the defining features of different animal groups, such as insects, birds, mammals, fish, and amphibians.
Questions that test knowledge of the defining features of plants and fungi, including how they obtain food, reproduce, and are structured.
Questions that cover the universal characteristics of living things, the distinction between living and non-living, and the properties of micro-organisms like bacteria.
